Echinacea parryi is a perennial plant species in the daisy family, known for its medicinal properties and traditional use in herbal medicine. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include immune system support, anti-inflammatory properties, and its ability to reduce the severity and duration of colds and flu. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea parryi include alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Echinacea parryi is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and ointments.
